steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the vitamin C content of brussels sprouts and apples. The correct answer must explicitly compare both foods on that specific nutrient.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Brussels sprouts contain vitamin C (also known as ascorbic acid)—80 mg in every 100 g, to be exact.": States a fact about one food but does not compare it to the other. ✗
  • "Brussels sprouts and apples both contain vitamin C, but brussels sprouts contain more.": Directly compares both foods on vitamin C content. ✓
  • "Brussels sprouts are vegetables, and apples are fruits.": Groups foods into categories rather than comparing their vitamin C levels. ✗
  • "Humans cannot make ascorbic acid in their bodies, but they can get it from brussels sprouts.": Addresses where vitamin C comes from, not how the two foods compare. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
